name: Meganova

dataResidency.euRegionDetails: Public docs state the company is based in the United States and that information may be transferred to and processed in the U.S. and other countries. Enterprise docs mention data residency choices and custom regions, but no public primary-source document guarantees EU-only processing for the standard API or confirms an EU region.

dataUsage.retentionPolicy: Privacy Policy says personal information is retained as long as necessary to provide the services and for legitimate purposes such as business/financial records, legal compliance, disputes/enforcement, safety, security, auditing, and product improvement. Separately, the security FAQ says prompts and outputs are not stored beyond temporary processing.
dataUsage.details: Privacy Policy says they collect content you upload or generate, plus usage data/logs, and use personal information to provide services and to improve, research, and develop the services, including analytics, quality assurance, safety systems, testing, anonymization/aggregation, and developing new features. No primary-source statement was found that customer/API data is used to train models, and no training opt-out was found.

notes: Meganova’s public docs identify the provider as Nebula Nova Inc. in the US, with cross-border transfers to the US and SCC-based safeguards where required. Public documentation does not expose a DPA/AVV or sub-processor list, and it does not publicly confirm whether API/customer data is used for model training. Enterprise pages mention data residency choices and SOC 2 Type II, but no public source guarantees EU-only inference routing for the standard API.
